    elif block.type == &quot;bash_code_execution_tool_result&quot;:
        print(f&quot;stdout: {block.content.stdout}&quot;)
</code></pre>
<h3>Upload Files for Analysis</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python"># 1. Upload a file
uploaded = client.beta.files.upload(file=open(&quot;sales_data.csv&quot;, &quot;rb&quot;))

# 2. Pass to code execution via container_upload block
# Code execution is GA; Files API is still beta (pass via extra_headers)
response = client.messages.create(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=4096,
    extra_headers={&quot;anthropic-beta&quot;: &quot;files-api-2025-04-14&quot;},
    messages=[{
        &quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;,
        &quot;content&quot;: [
            {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;text&quot;, &quot;text&quot;: &quot;Analyze this sales data. Show trends and create a visualization.&quot;},
            {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;container_upload&quot;, &quot;file_id&quot;: uploaded.id}
        ]
    }],
    tools=[{&quot;type&quot;: &quot;code_execution_20260120&quot;, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;code_execution&quot;}]
)
</code></pre>
<h3>Retrieve Generated Files</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python">import os

OUTPUT_DIR = &quot;./claude_outputs&quot;
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

for block in response.content:
    if block.type == &quot;bash_code_execution_tool_result&quot;:
        result = block.content
        if result.type == &quot;bash_code_execution_result&quot; and result.content:
            for file_ref in result.content:
                if file_ref.type == &quot;bash_code_execution_output&quot;:
                    metadata = client.beta.files.retrieve_metadata(file_ref.file_id)
                    file_content = client.beta.files.download(file_ref.file_id)
                    # Use basename to prevent path traversal; validate result
                    safe_name = os.path.basename(metadata.filename)
                    if not safe_name or safe_name in (&quot;.&quot;, &quot;..&quot;):
                        print(f&quot;Skipping invalid filename: {metadata.filename}&quot;)
                        continue
                    output_path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, safe_name)
                    file_content.write_to_file(output_path)
                    print(f&quot;Saved: {output_path}&quot;)
</code></pre>
<h3>Container Reuse</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python"># First request: set up environment
response1 = client.messages.create(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=4096,
    messages=[{&quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;, &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Install tabulate and create data.json with sample data&quot;}],
    tools=[{&quot;type&quot;: &quot;code_execution_20260120&quot;, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;code_execution&quot;}]
)

# Get container ID from response
container_id = response1.container.id

# Second request: reuse the same container
response2 = client.messages.create(
    container=container_id,
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=4096,
    messages=[{&quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;, &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Read data.json and display as a formatted table&quot;}],
    tools=[{&quot;type&quot;: &quot;code_execution_20260120&quot;, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;code_execution&quot;}]
)
</code></pre>
<h3>Response Structure</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python">for block in response.content:
    if block.type == &quot;text&quot;:
        print(block.text)  # Claude's explanation
    elif block.type == &quot;server_tool_use&quot;:
        print(f&quot;Running: {block.name} - {block.input}&quot;)  # What Claude is doing
    elif block.type == &quot;bash_code_execution_tool_result&quot;:
        result = block.content
        if result.type == &quot;bash_code_execution_result&quot;:
            if result.return_code == 0:
                print(f&quot;Output: {result.stdout}&quot;)
            else:
                print(f&quot;Error: {result.stderr}&quot;)
        else:
            print(f&quot;Tool error: {result.error_code}&quot;)
    elif block.type == &quot;text_editor_code_execution_tool_result&quot;:
        print(f&quot;File operation: {block.content}&quot;)
</code></pre>
<hr />
<h2>Memory Tool</h2>
<h3>Basic Usage</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python">import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

response = client.messages.create(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=2048,
    messages=[{&quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;, &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Remember that my preferred language is Python.&quot;}],
    tools=[{&quot;type&quot;: &quot;memory_20250818&quot;, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;memory&quot;}],
)
</code></pre>
<h3>SDK Memory Helper</h3>
<p>Subclass <code>BetaAbstractMemoryTool</code>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-python">from anthropic.lib.tools import BetaAbstractMemoryTool

class MyMemoryTool(BetaAbstractMemoryTool):
    def view(self, command): ...
    def create(self, command): ...
    def str_replace(self, command): ...
    def insert(self, command): ...
    def delete(self, command): ...
    def rename(self, command): ...

memory = MyMemoryTool()

# Use with tool runner
runner = client.beta.messages.tool_runner(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=2048,
    tools=[memory],
    messages=[{&quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;, &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Remember my preferences&quot;}],
)

for message in runner:
    print(message)
</code></pre>
<p>For full implementation examples, use WebFetch:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python/blob/main/examples/memory/basic.py</code></li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h2>Structured Outputs</h2>
<h3>JSON Outputs (Pydantic — Recommended)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python">from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List
import anthropic

class ContactInfo(BaseModel):
    name: str
    email: str
    plan: str
    interests: List[str]
    demo_requested: bool

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

response = client.messages.parse(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{
        &quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;,
        &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Extract: Jane Doe (jane@co.com) wants Enterprise, interested in API and SDKs, wants a demo.&quot;
    }],
    output_format=ContactInfo,
)

# response.parsed_output is a validated ContactInfo instance
contact = response.parsed_output
print(contact.name)           # &quot;Jane Doe&quot;
print(contact.interests)      # [&quot;API&quot;, &quot;SDKs&quot;]
</code></pre>
<h3>Raw Schema</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python">response = client.messages.create(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{
        &quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;,
        &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Extract info: John Smith (john@example.com) wants the Enterprise plan.&quot;
    }],
    output_config={
        &quot;format&quot;: {
            &quot;type&quot;: &quot;json_schema&quot;,
            &quot;schema&quot;: {
                &quot;type&quot;: &quot;object&quot;,
                &quot;properties&quot;: {
                    &quot;name&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;},
                    &quot;email&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;},
                    &quot;plan&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;},
                    &quot;demo_requested&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;boolean&quot;}
                },
                &quot;required&quot;: [&quot;name&quot;, &quot;email&quot;, &quot;plan&quot;, &quot;demo_requested&quot;],
                &quot;additionalProperties&quot;: False
            }
        }
    }
)

import json
data = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
</code></pre>
<h3>Strict Tool Use</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python">response = client.messages.create(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{&quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;, &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Book a flight to Tokyo for 2 passengers on March 15&quot;}],
    tools=[{
        &quot;name&quot;: &quot;book_flight&quot;,
        &quot;description&quot;: &quot;Book a flight to a destination&quot;,
        &quot;strict&quot;: True,
        &quot;input_schema&quot;: {
            &quot;type&quot;: &quot;object&quot;,
            &quot;properties&quot;: {
                &quot;destination&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;},
                &quot;date&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;, &quot;format&quot;: &quot;date&quot;},
                &quot;passengers&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;integer&quot;, &quot;enum&quot;: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]}
            },
            &quot;required&quot;: [&quot;destination&quot;, &quot;date&quot;, &quot;passengers&quot;],
            &quot;additionalProperties&quot;: False
        }
    }]
)
</code></pre>
<h3>Using Both Together</h3>
<pre><code class="language-python">response = client.messages.create(
    model=&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{&quot;role&quot;: &quot;user&quot;, &quot;content&quot;: &quot;Plan a trip to Paris next month&quot;}],
    output_config={
        &quot;format&quot;: {
            &quot;type&quot;: &quot;json_schema&quot;,
            &quot;schema&quot;: {
                &quot;type&quot;: &quot;object&quot;,
                &quot;properties&quot;: {
                    &quot;summary&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;},
                    &quot;next_steps&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;array&quot;, &quot;items&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;}}
                },
                &quot;required&quot;: [&quot;summary&quot;, &quot;next_steps&quot;],
                &quot;additionalProperties&quot;: False
            }
        }
    },
    tools=[{
        &quot;name&quot;: &quot;search_flights&quot;,
        &quot;description&quot;: &quot;Search for available flights&quot;,
        &quot;strict&quot;: True,
        &quot;input_schema&quot;: {
            &quot;type&quot;: &quot;object&quot;,
            &quot;properties&quot;: {
                &quot;destination&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;},
                &quot;date&quot;: {&quot;type&quot;: &quot;string&quot;, &quot;format&quot;: &quot;date&quot;}
            },
            &quot;required&quot;: [&quot;destination&quot;, &quot;date&quot;],
            &quot;additionalProperties&quot;: False
        }
    }]
)
</code></pre>
</doc>

<doc path="ruby/claude-api.md">
<h1>Claude API — Ruby</h1>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Note:</strong> The Ruby SDK supports the Claude API. A tool runner is available in beta via <code>client.beta.messages.tool_runner()</code>. Agent SDK is not yet available for Ruby.</p>
</blockquote>
<h2>Installation</h2>
<pre><code class="language-bash">gem install anthropic
</code></pre>
<h2>Client Initialization</h2>
<pre><code class="language-ruby">require &quot;anthropic&quot;

# Default (uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var)
client = Anthropic::Client.new

# Explicit API key
client = Anthropic::Client.new(api_key: &quot;your-api-key&quot;)
</code></pre>
<hr />
<h2>Basic Message Request</h2>
<pre><code class="language-ruby">message = client.messages.create(
  model: :&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
  max_tokens: 1024,
  messages: [
    { role: &quot;user&quot;, content: &quot;What is the capital of France?&quot; }
  ]
)
puts message.content.first.text
</code></pre>
<hr />
<h2>Streaming</h2>
<pre><code class="language-ruby">stream = client.messages.stream(
  model: :&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
  max_tokens: 1024,
  messages: [{ role: &quot;user&quot;, content: &quot;Write a haiku&quot; }]
)

stream.text.each { |text| print(text) }
</code></pre>
<hr />
<h2>Tool Use</h2>
<p>The Ruby SDK supports tool use via raw JSON schema definitions and also provides a beta tool runner for automatic tool execution.</p>
<h3>Tool Runner (Beta)</h3>
<pre><code class="language-ruby">class GetWeatherInput &lt; Anthropic::BaseModel
  required :location, String, doc: &quot;City and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA&quot;
end

class GetWeather &lt; Anthropic::BaseTool
  doc &quot;Get the current weather for a location&quot;

  input_schema GetWeatherInput

  def call(input)
    &quot;The weather in #{input.location} is sunny and 72°F.&quot;
  end
end

client.beta.messages.tool_runner(
  model: :&quot;claude-opus-4-6&quot;,
  max_tokens: 1024,
  tools: [GetWeather.new],
  messages: [{ role: &quot;user&quot;, content: &quot;What's the weather in San Francisco?&quot; }]
).each_message do |message|
  puts message.content
end
</code></pre>
<h3>Manual Loop</h3>
<p>See the <a href="../shared/tool-use-concepts.md">shared tool use concepts</a> for the tool definition format and agentic loop pattern.</p>
</doc>

<doc path="shared/error-codes.md">
<h1>HTTP Error Codes Reference</h1>
<p>This file documents HTTP error codes returned by the Claude API, their common causes, and how to handle them. For language-specific error handling examples, see the <code>python/</code> or <code>typescript/</code> folders.</p>
<h2>Error Code Summary</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr><th>Code</th><th>Error Type</th><th>Retryable</th><th>Common Cause</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>400</td><td><code>invalid_request_error</code></td><td>No</td><td>Invalid request format or parameters</td></tr>
<tr><td>401</td><td><code>authentication_error</code></td><td>No</td><td>Invalid or missing API key</td></tr>
<tr><td>403</td><td><code>permission_error</code></td><td>No</td><td>API key lacks permission</td></tr>
<tr><td>404</td><td><code>not_found_error</code></td><td>No</td><td>Invalid endpoint or model ID</td></tr>
<tr><td>413</td><td><code>request_too_large</code></td><td>No</td><td>Request exceeds size limits</td></tr>
<tr><td>429</td><td><code>rate_limit_error</code></td><td>Yes</td><td>Too many requests</td></tr>
<tr><td>500</td><td><code>api_error</code></td><td>Yes</td><td>Anthropic service issue</td></tr>
<tr><td>529</td><td><code>overloaded_error</code></td><td>Yes</td><td>API is temporarily overloaded</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>Detailed Error Information</h2>
<h3>400 Bad Request</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Malformed JSON in request body</li>
<li>Missing required parameters (<code>model</code>, <code>max_tokens</code>, <code>messages</code>)</li>
<li>Invalid parameter types (e.g., string where integer expected)</li>
<li>Empty messages array</li>
<li>Messages not alternating user/assistant</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Example error:</strong></p>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  &quot;type&quot;: &quot;error&quot;,
  &quot;error&quot;: {
    &quot;type&quot;: &quot;invalid_request_error&quot;,
    &quot;message&quot;: &quot;messages: roles must alternate between \&quot;user\&quot; and \&quot;assistant\&quot;&quot;
  }
}
</code></pre>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> Validate request structure before sending. Check that:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>model</code> is a valid model ID</li>
<li><code>max_tokens</code> is a positive integer</li>
<li><code>messages</code> array is non-empty and alternates correctly</li>
</ul>
<hr />
<h3>401 Unauthorized</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Missing <code>x-api-key</code> header or <code>Authorization</code> header</li>
<li>Invalid API key format</li>
<li>Revoked or deleted API key</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> Ensure <code>ANTHROPIC_API_KEY</code> environment variable is set correctly.</p>
<hr />
<h3>403 Forbidden</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>API key doesn't have access to the requested model</li>
<li>Organization-level restrictions</li>
<li>Attempting to access beta features without beta access</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> Check your API key permissions in the Console. You may need a different API key or to request access to specific features.</p>
<hr />
<h3>404 Not Found</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Typo in model ID (e.g., <code>claude-sonnet-4.6</code> instead of <code>claude-sonnet-4-6</code>)</li>
<li>Using deprecated model ID</li>
<li>Invalid API endpoint</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> Use exact model IDs from the models documentation. You can use aliases (e.g., <code>claude-opus-4-6</code>).</p>
<hr />
<h3>413 Request Too Large</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Request body exceeds maximum size</li>
<li>Too many tokens in input</li>
<li>Image data too large</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> Reduce input size — truncate conversation history, compress/resize images, or split large documents into chunks.</p>
<hr />
<h3>400 Validation Errors</h3>
<p>Some 400 errors are specifically related to parameter validation:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>max_tokens</code> exceeds model's limit</li>
<li>Invalid <code>temperature</code> value (must be 0.0-1.0)</li>
<li><code>budget_tokens</code> &gt;= <code>max_tokens</code> in extended thinking</li>
<li>Invalid tool definition schema</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Common mistake with extended thinking:</strong></p>
<pre><code># Wrong: budget_tokens must be &lt; max_tokens
thinking: budget_tokens=10000, max_tokens=1000  → Error!

# Correct
thinking: budget_tokens=10000, max_tokens=16000
</code></pre>
<hr />
<h3>429 Rate Limited</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Exceeded requests per minute (RPM)</li>
<li>Exceeded tokens per minute (TPM)</li>
<li>Exceeded tokens per day (TPD)</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Headers to check:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><code>retry-after</code>: Seconds to wait before retrying</li>
<li><code>x-ratelimit-limit-*</code>: Your limits</li>
<li><code>x-ratelimit-remaining-*</code>: Remaining quota</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> The Anthropic SDKs automatically retry 429 and 5xx errors with exponential backoff (default: <code>max_retries=2</code>). For custom retry behavior, see the language-specific error handling examples.</p>
<hr />
<h3>500 Internal Server Error</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Temporary Anthropic service issue</li>
<li>Bug in API processing</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> Retry with exponential backoff. If persistent, check <a href="https://status.anthropic.com">status.anthropic.com</a>.</p>
<hr />
<h3>529 Overloaded</h3>
<p><strong>Causes:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>High API demand</li>
<li>Service capacity reached</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Fix:</strong> Retry with exponential backoff. Consider using a different model (Haiku is often less loaded), spreading requests over time, or implementing request queuing.</p>
<hr />
<h2>Common Mistakes and Fixes</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr><th>Mistake</th><th>Error</th><th>Fix</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td><code>budget_tokens</code> &gt;= <code>max_tokens</code></td><td>400</td><td>Ensure <code>budget_tokens</code> &lt; <code>max_tokens</code></td></tr>
<tr><td>Typo in model ID</td><td>404</td><td>Use valid model ID like <code>claude-opus-4-6</code></td></tr>
<tr><td>First message is <code>assistant</code></td><td>400</td><td>First message must be <code>user</code></td></tr>
<tr><td>Consecutive same-role messages</td><td>400</td><td>Alternate <code>user</code> and <code>assistant</code></td></tr>
<tr><td>API key in code</td><td>401 (leaked key)</td><td>Use environment variable</td></tr>
<tr><td>Custom retry needs</td><td>429/5xx</td><td>SDK retries automatically; customize with <code>max_retries</code></td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</doc>

<doc path="shared/live-sources.md">
<h1>Live Documentation Sources</h1>
<p>This file contains WebFetch URLs for fetching current information from platform.claude.com and Agent SDK repositories. Use these when users need the latest data that may have changed since the cached content was last updated.</p>
<h2>When to Use WebFetch</h2>
<ul>
<li>User explicitly asks for &quot;latest&quot; or &quot;current&quot; information</li>
<li>Cached data seems incorrect</li>
<li>User asks about features not covered in cached content</li>
<li>User needs specific API details or examples</li>
</ul>
<h2>Claude API Documentation URLs</h2>
<h3>Models &amp; Pricing</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr><th>Topic</th><th>URL</th><th>Extraction Prompt</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>Models Overview</td><td><code>https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview.md</code></td><td>&quot;Extract current model IDs, context windows, and pricing for all Claude models&quot;</td></tr>
<tr><td>Pricing</td><td><code>https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/pricing.md</code></td><td>&quot;Extract current pricing per million tokens for input and output&quot;</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<doc path="shared/models.md">
<h1>Claude Model Catalog</h1>
<p><strong>Only use exact model IDs listed in this file.</strong> Never guess or construct model IDs — incorrect IDs will cause API errors. Use aliases wherever available. For the latest information, WebFetch the Models Overview URL in <code>shared/live-sources.md</code>.</p>
<h2>Current Models (recommended)</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr><th>Friendly Name</th><th>Alias (use this)</th><th>Full ID</th><th>Context</th><th>Max Output</th><th>Status</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>Claude Opus 4.6</td><td><code>claude-opus-4-6</code></td><td>—</td><td>200K (1M beta)</td><td>128K</td><td>Active</td></tr>
<tr><td>Claude Sonnet 4.6</td><td><code>claude-sonnet-4-6</code></td><td>-</td><td>200K (1M beta)</td><td>64K</td><td>Active</td></tr>
<tr><td>Claude Haiku 4.5</td><td><code>claude-haiku-4-5</code></td><td><code>claude-haiku-4-5-20251001</code></td><td>200K</td><td>64K</td><td>Active</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3>Model Descriptions</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Claude Opus 4.6</strong> — Our most intelligent model for building agents and coding. Supports adaptive thinking (recommended), 128K max output tokens (requires streaming for large outputs). 1M context window available in beta via <code>context-1m-2025-08-07</code> header.</li>
<li><strong>Claude Sonnet 4.6</strong> — Our best combination of speed and intelligence. Supports adaptive thinking (recommended). 1M context window available in beta via <code>context-1m-2025-08-07</code> header. 64K max output tokens.</li>
<li><strong>Claude Haiku 4.5</strong> — Fastest and most cost-effective model for simple tasks.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Legacy Models (still active)</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr><th>Friendly Name</th><th>Alias (use this)</th><th>Full ID</th><th>Status</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>Claude Opus 4.5</td><td><code>claude-opus-4-5</code></td><td><code>claude-opus-4-5-20251101</code></td><td>Active</td></tr>
<tr><td>Claude Opus 4.1</td><td><code>claude-opus-4-1</code></td><td><code>claude-opus-4-1-20250805</code></td><td>Active</td></tr>
<tr><td>Claude Sonnet 4.5</td><td><code>claude-sonnet-4-5</code></td><td><code>claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929</code></td><td>Active</td></tr>
<tr><td>Claude Sonnet 4</td><td><code>claude-sonnet-4-0</code></td><td><code>claude-sonnet-4-20250514</code></td><td>Active</td></tr>
<tr><td>Claude Opus 4</td><td><code>claude-opus-4-0</code></td><td><code>claude-opus-4-20250514</code></td><td>Active</td></tr>
<tr><td>Claude Haiku 3</td><td>—</td><td><code>claude-3-haiku-20240307</code></td><td>Active</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2>Deprecated Models (retiring soon)</h2>
<p>(none currently)</p>

<thead>
<tr><th>Friendly Name</th><th>Full ID</th><th>Retired</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>

<h2>Resolving User Requests</h2>

<tr><th>User says...</th><th>Use this model ID</th></tr>
</thead>
